medical_records.chief_complaint: Increasing migraine frequency — 6-8 attacks per month over past 3 months
medical_records.assessment: Episodic migraine with aura. Frequency increase likely linked to new stressors and irregular sleep. No red flag features. Neurological exam normal.

medications.name: Sumatriptan
medications.instructions: Take at onset of aura or headache. May repeat once after 2 hours. Max 2 doses per 24 hours.

medical_records.plan: Trigger diary for 8 weeks. Prioritize sleep consistency. Return if frequency exceeds 8 days per month — will discuss preventive medication at that threshold.

medical_records.chief_complaint: Follow-up migraine management. Frequency stable at 5-6 per month.
medical_records.assessment: Good response to acute triptan therapy. Trigger diary confirms barometric pressure drops and poor sleep as primary triggers. Frequency below preventive threshold.
medical_records.plan: Continue current regimen. Patient educated on weather trigger patterns and early intervention. Wearable HRV data reviewed — low HRV nights correlate with attack days.

medical_records.chief_complaint: Routine follow-up. Reports 2 severe attacks in August — both preceded by storm fronts.
medical_records.assessment: Stable episodic migraine. Weather sensitivity well-established. No change in aura pattern. August attacks severe but self-limited with sumatriptan.
medical_records.plan: Monitor barometric pressure app. Pre-treat with additional hydration and reduced screen time on pressure-drop days. Check magnesium level to confirm supplementation is adequate.

medical_records.chief_complaint: Annual physical. Fatigue and increased thirst for past 6 months.
medical_records.assessment: New diagnosis of type 2 diabetes based on fasting glucose 142 mg/dL and HbA1c 8.2%. Concurrent stage 1 hypertension. BMI 29.4.

medications.name: Metformin
medications.instructions: Once daily with dinner. Titrate to 1000mg twice daily over 4 weeks if tolerated.

medical_records.plan: Diabetes education referral. Low-glycemic diet counseling. 150 min moderate exercise per week target. Recheck HbA1c in 3 months.

medical_records.chief_complaint: 3-month diabetes follow-up. HbA1c recheck.
medical_records.assessment: HbA1c improved to 7.4%. Weight down 5 lbs. Metformin well-tolerated at full dose. GI side effects resolved. BP improving but still above target.
medical_records.plan: Continue titrated Metformin. Add SGLT-2 inhibitor if HbA1c remains above 7% at next visit. Reinforce dietary carbohydrate reduction and consistent meal timing.

medical_records.chief_complaint: 6-month diabetes review. HbA1c still above 7%.
medical_records.assessment: HbA1c 7.1%. Marginal improvement. Shift work disrupting meal timing and sleep — likely blunting medication benefit. BP well-controlled.

medications.name: Empagliflozin
medications.instructions: Once daily in the morning. Monitor for UTI symptoms.

medical_records.plan: Added SGLT-2 inhibitor for additional glucose lowering and cardiovascular protection. Discuss sleep and meal timing strategies for shift workers. Target HbA1c below 7%.

medical_records.chief_complaint: Annual diabetes review. Patient reports feeling better overall.
medical_records.assessment: HbA1c 6.9% — first time below 7%. Combined Metformin + Empagliflozin working well. Weight down 10 lbs from baseline. BP at target.
medical_records.plan: Maintain current regimen. Continue monitoring glucose patterns around shift work days — data shows spikes on days after night shifts. Next HbA1c in 6 months.

medical_records.chief_complaint: Persistent fatigue, cognitive difficulties, and unrefreshing sleep for 4 months following COVID-19 infection.
medical_records.assessment: Presentation consistent with post-viral fatigue syndrome / early ME/CFS. Orthostatic tachycardia noted on standing test (HR increase 28 bpm). Cognitive testing shows reduced processing speed.
medical_records.plan: Strict pacing and energy envelope management. Graded exercise contraindicated at this stage. Refer to ME/CFS specialist. Follow up with lab results in 4 weeks.

medical_records.chief_complaint: Lab results review. Fatigue unchanged.
medical_records.assessment: Labs unremarkable except low-normal ferritin (18 ng/mL) and Vitamin D insufficiency (24 ng/mL). No secondary cause for fatigue identified. ME/CFS diagnosis confirmed per Canadian Consensus Criteria.

medications.name: Low-dose naltrexone
medications.instructions: Once nightly. Titrate from 1.5mg over 6 weeks.

medications.name: Ferrous sulfate
medications.instructions: Every other day to improve absorption. Take with Vitamin C.

medical_records.plan: LDN trial for 3 months — may reduce neuroinflammation and improve sleep quality. Strict activity pacing remains cornerstone of management. Heart rate monitoring to stay below anaerobic threshold.

medical_records.chief_complaint: 6-month check-in. Mild improvement in sleep, PEM still significant.
medical_records.assessment: Partial response to LDN. Sleep quality improved per patient report and wearable data. Post-exertional malaise remains the dominant symptom — any activity above moderate threshold triggers 2-3 day crash.
medical_records.plan: Reinforce heart rate ceiling of 110 bpm during any activity. Sleep consistency critical — irregular sleep timing worsens PEM severity. Cognitive pacing equally important as physical pacing.

medical_records.chief_complaint: Annual review. Stable but not improving further.
medical_records.assessment: ME/CFS stable at moderate severity. No deterioration over past 12 months. PEM pattern predictable — overexertion events cluster around social and travel commitments.
medical_records.plan: Continue current regimen. Patient educated on pre-activity planning to budget energy expenditure. Wearable HRV and resting HR useful early warning signals for impending crash.

medical_records.chief_complaint: Loud snoring, daytime sleepiness, non-restorative sleep. Partner reports witnessed apneas.
medical_records.assessment: High clinical suspicion for OSA. Epworth Sleepiness Scale 14/24. Overnight oximetry shows significant desaturation events. Neck circumference 17.5 inches.
medical_records.plan: Home sleep study ordered. Avoid alcohol within 3 hours of bed pending diagnosis. Sleep position therapy (avoid supine) as interim measure.

medical_records.chief_complaint: HSAT results review.
medical_records.assessment: HSAT confirms moderate-severe OSA. AHI 22.4 events/hour. Oxygen nadir 84%. CPAP therapy indicated.

medications.name: CPAP therapy
medications.instructions: Use every night. Target 7+ hours. Download compliance data monthly.

medical_records.plan: CPAP setup with DME provider. Follow-up in 4 weeks for compliance and efficacy review. Weight loss strongly encouraged — 10% body weight reduction can reduce AHI by 26%.

medical_records.chief_complaint: Annual CPAP review. Reports feeling much better on therapy.
medical_records.assessment: CPAP compliance 78% of nights (usage >4h). Residual AHI 3.1 on therapy — excellent control. BP improved significantly since OSA treatment. Weight down 7 lbs.
diagnoses: Obstructive sleep apnea, controlled on CPAP (G47.33)

medications.name: CPAP therapy
medications.instructions: Titrated to fixed pressure based on 12-month auto data. Continue nightly.

medical_records.plan: Increase CPAP compliance target to 85%+ of nights. Correlation between non-compliance nights and next-day headache and fatigue is clear in data. Continue weight management.

medical_records.chief_complaint: Persistent excessive worry, muscle tension, sleep onset difficulty for over 1 year. PHQ-9: 8. GAD-7: 14.
medical_records.assessment: Meets DSM-5 criteria for Generalized Anxiety Disorder. Moderate severity. No evidence of bipolar or psychotic features. Sleep disturbance secondary to anxiety rumination.

medications.name: Venlafaxine
medications.instructions: Once daily with food. Titrate to 75mg after 2 weeks if tolerated.

medical_records.plan: SNRI initiated. CBT referral placed — 12-session program. Review in 6 weeks. Avoid abrupt discontinuation of venlafaxine.

medical_records.chief_complaint: Medication review. Partial improvement. Still significant anxiety around work deadlines.
medical_records.assessment: GAD-7 reduced from 14 to 10. Partial response to venlafaxine 75mg. Sleep improving. Situational anxiety spikes remain prominent, particularly around deadlines.

medications.name: Propranolol
medications.instructions: As needed for acute anxiety situations — presentations, high-stress events. Max once daily.

medical_records.plan: Add propranolol PRN for situational anxiety. Continue CBT. Wearable HRV monitoring useful for detecting anxiety build-up before it becomes symptomatic.

medical_records.chief_complaint: Annual review. Well-controlled anxiety. Asks about medication taper.
medical_records.assessment: GAD-7 score 6 — mild. Excellent response to combined venlafaxine and CBT. Sleep consistent. HRV trending up over past 6 months per wearable data. Discusses taper — advised to wait 12 more stable months.

medications.name: Venlafaxine
medications.instructions: No change. Do not taper without medical supervision.

medical_records.plan: Maintain current regimen for minimum 12 months given recent stability. Early warning signs to watch for: sleep deterioration, HRV drop, increased resting HR over baseline — all precede anxiety flares by 2-3 days.

medical_records.chief_complaint: Routine physical. No complaints. Hypertension found incidentally.
medical_records.assessment: Stage 1-2 hypertension on repeat measurement. No evidence of end-organ damage. No secondary causes identified. Metabolic panel normal except borderline LDL.

medications.name: Amlodipine
medications.instructions: Once daily. May cause ankle swelling — monitor.

medical_records.plan: DASH diet counseling. Sodium target under 2g per day. Reduce alcohol. 30 min aerobic exercise 5x per week. Recheck in 6 weeks.

medical_records.chief_complaint: BP recheck. Patient reports dietary changes, less alcohol.
medical_records.assessment: Partial BP response to amlodipine and lifestyle. Still above target 130/80. Add ARB for better control.

medications.name: Losartan
medications.instructions: Once daily. Monitor potassium. Avoid NSAIDs.

lab_orders: eGFR in 2 weeks after ARB start
medical_records.plan: Combination therapy. 24-hour ambulatory BP monitoring in 3 months to assess true BP burden including nighttime dipping pattern.

medical_records.chief_complaint: Annual physical. BP well-controlled per home readings.
medical_records.assessment: BP at target on dual therapy. Weight down 7 lbs. LDL improved to 128 mg/dL. No end-organ damage on ECG or urine. 24h ambulatory showed non-dipping pattern — BP does not fall adequately at night.

medications.name: Losartan
medications.instructions: Dose increase to improve nocturnal dipping. Take in evening.

medical_records.plan: Losartan moved to evening dosing to improve nocturnal BP dipping. Sleep quality directly impacts BP control — poor sleep nights show BP spikes next morning.

medical_records.chief_complaint: Annual physical. Reports occasional morning headaches.
medical_records.assessment: BP averaging 132/84 — slightly above target. Morning headaches consistent with nocturnal hypertension. Repeat 24h ambulatory shows improvement in dipping but still non-dipper pattern.
medical_records.plan: Reinforce sodium restriction — still highest modifiable risk factor. Wearable data shows BP correlates closely with sleep quality and stress scores. Next visit consider adding spironolactone if still above target.

medical_records.chief_complaint: Acute wheezing and chest tightness during outdoor run. Used albuterol with partial relief.
medical_records.assessment: Acute asthma exacerbation, mild-moderate. SpO2 95% improved to 98% post-bronchodilator. AQI was 112 on day of event. Exercise-induced component likely. Spirometry shows FEV1/FVC 0.72.

medications.name: Albuterol inhaler
medications.instructions: 2 puffs as needed for symptoms. Use 15 min before exercise.

medications.name: Fluticasone inhaled
medications.instructions: 2 puffs twice daily as controller. Rinse mouth after.

lab_orders: Allergen panel (dust mites, mold, grass pollen)
medical_records.plan: Initiate ICS controller therapy. Avoid outdoor exercise when AQI above 100. Pre-exercise bronchodilator use mandatory. Follow up in 6 weeks.

medical_records.chief_complaint: Asthma follow-up. Improved on ICS. Still occasional wheeze on cold mornings.
medical_records.assessment: Good response to ICS. Rescue inhaler use down from daily to 2x per week. Allergen panel: positive for grass pollen and dust mites. Cold air trigger confirmed by patient history.
medical_records.plan: Asthma well-controlled on current regimen. Allergen avoidance counseling for dust mites. Scarf over mouth in cold air. Monitor AQI daily — threshold for outdoor activity is AQI under 100.

medical_records.chief_complaint: Annual athlete physical. No complaints. Pre-season clearance.
medical_records.assessment: Excellent cardiovascular fitness. Resting HR 48 bpm consistent with athletic conditioning. VO2 max estimated 58 mL/kg/min. No musculoskeletal issues. Occasional fatigue during heavy training blocks — within normal range.
medical_records.plan: No concerns. Ensure adequate caloric and protein intake during training blocks. Monitor HRV — sustained HRV drop over 5+ days indicates overtraining. Rest week protocol if HRV drops 15% from baseline.

medical_records.chief_complaint: Annual physical. Reports low energy, poor sleep, and weight gain over past year.
medical_records.assessment: No active conditions. Borderline low HRV on wearable data submitted. BMI 27.8 — overweight range. Sedentary lifestyle confirmed (avg 3,800 steps/day by wearable). Sleep 6h average.
medical_records.plan: Lifestyle intervention counseling. Target 7,000 steps per day as starting goal. Sleep hygiene education — 7-8h target. Recheck labs in 12 months. Flag if fasting glucose trends toward prediabetes range.
